title: Surgical cleanup
prompt: rm deletes files, and there is NO recycle bin — read twice, run once. The lab has one keeper (keep.txt) plus junk: temp1.tmp, temp2.tmp, temp3.tmp, and a cache/ folder. Delete the three .tmp files and the cache folder. keep.txt must survive. Folders need rm -r.

checks.command: test -d cache && echo present || echo gone

details.restate: Delete exactly four things: the three .tmp files and the cache folder with everything in it. keep.txt stays untouched. If you delete the wrong thing, use ⟲ reset lab.

terms.meaning: delete a folder AND everything inside it (r = recursive); plain rm only handles files

help.plain: Plain rm removes the three .tmp files; the folder needs the recursive form.
help.analogy: Shredding three loose papers is easy — shredding a whole binder means feeding in every page it contains, which is what -r does.
help.mnemonic: files: rm · folders: rm -r · no undo, ever.
help.nudge: rm can take several filenames in one command. What extra letter does a folder need?
